topic: high altitude, slow ripening, acidity, and aromatic freshness
trigger_keywords: efecto tiene la altitud en los vinos del Valle de Uco
aumenta la acidez y retención de aromas
factor natural permite a Salta producir vinos aromáticos

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A vineyard is planted at high elevation, where ambient temperatures, especially at night, are lower.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Cooler conditions slow ripening and reduce the respiratory loss of grape acids while extending aromatic development.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: High-altitude fruit can retain greater acidity and aromatic freshness while ripening more slowly than comparable lower-altitude fruit.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: High-altitude fruit can retain greater acidity and aromatic freshness while ripening more slowly than comparable lower-altitude fruit.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
